articles.title: A Giant Cosmic Sheet Around Our Galaxy
articles.summary: Astronomers have found a giant flat sheet of matter around the Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies. This sheet is made of both normal matter and dark matter, which we cannot see. It stretches for tens of millions of light-years. The sheet helps explain why many nearby galaxies are moving away from us instead of being pulled in by gravity. Scientists used computer simulations to create a virtual twin of our cosmic neighborhood. They started with conditions from the early universe and evolved it to match today. The simulations show that the sheet's mass balances the gravity of the Local Group, which includes the Milky Way and Andromeda. This solves a mystery that puzzled astronomers for about fifty years. The discovery is exciting because it uses galaxy motions to understand mass distribution.

articles.title: Electrons Zoom Super Fast in Solar Materials
articles.summary: Scientists found that electrons can move very quickly across solar materials, which helps make solar panels better at turning sunlight into electricity. In experiments, they watched electrons travel in just 18 femtoseconds, which is an incredibly short time—like a tiny fraction of a second. The electrons don't drift slowly; they get launched like from a catapult by vibrations in the molecules. This discovery is exciting because it challenges old ideas about how solar energy works and could lead to more efficient solar cells that capture more sunlight. The research was done at the University of Cambridge and shows that vibrations actively push the electrons, making them move faster than expected. This could help design new technologies to use solar power more effectively.

articles.title: AI Finds Liver Disease Early with a Simple Blood Test
articles.summary: Scientists at Johns Hopkins have created a new blood test that uses artificial intelligence (AI) to find liver disease before people feel sick. The test looks at tiny pieces of DNA floating in the blood, called cell-free DNA. By studying how these DNA pieces break and where they come from in the body, the AI can spot signs of liver problems like fibrosis and cirrhosis. This is important because liver disease can be reversed if caught early, but if not, it can lead to serious issues like liver cancer. The test works by analyzing millions of DNA fragments with machine learning, a type of AI that learns patterns. It could help millions of people who don't know they have liver disease, as current tests often miss early signs. This research was funded by the National Institutes of Health and published in a science journal.

articles.title: A Super Detailed Picture of the Milky Way's Center
articles.summary: Scientists have taken the most detailed picture ever of the center of our galaxy, the Milky Way. They used a big telescope called ALMA in Chile to see cold gas clouds that are invisible to our eyes. This gas is what stars are made from. The picture shows a busy area near a huge black hole, where stars form in extreme conditions. It helps astronomers learn how stars are born in this wild part of space. The image is very big, like three Moons side by side in the sky. This is just the start, as new telescopes will soon show even more details.

articles.title: New Penguins Join Wildlife Park
articles.summary: A wildlife park in Gloucestershire, UK, has welcomed three new Humboldt penguins. These penguins are male and came from Wales to join other penguins at the park. Humboldt penguins live in Peru and Chile in South America and are in danger because of problems like climate change and overfishing. The park helps protect these penguins by having them in a safe place where they can breed and live. Visitors can see the new penguins in the Penguin Shore area. The park also works with scientists to help penguins in the wild by building nest boxes and collecting data. This is part of a big effort to save the species.

articles.title: TerraPower Starts Building a New Nuclear Plant in Wyoming
articles.summary: TerraPower, a company backed by Bill Gates, has received permission to start building its first nuclear power plant in Kemmerer, Wyoming. This is a big step because it's the first new nuclear construction approved in the US in almost ten years. The plant, called Natrium, is special because it uses liquid sodium for cooling instead of water, which makes it safer and more efficient. It also has energy storage, so it can save heat to make electricity later, helping it work well with renewable energy like solar and wind. The plant is smaller than most nuclear plants and is expected to be finished by 2030. Before it can operate, it still needs another license, but starting construction is an important milestone for clean energy.
